summary: Backend-only end-to-end testing for TimberLog FastAPI app. Created comprehensive pytest suite covering auth, purchases CRUD, container measurements with CBM/CFT formula verification, dashboard summary with filters, cascade deletes, and per-user isolation. 25/25 tests passing against the public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL.

minor.issue: Empty string is allowed for required fields like supplier_name/country/bl_number on update because Pydantic only checks for None. Consider min_length validation.

Pydantic does not enforce min_length on required string fields (bl_number, supplier_name, country). Empty strings would pass schema validation; only the explicit `if not payload.containers` and uniqueness checks guard the endpoint.
N+1 query pattern: per-purchase loops issue separate find() calls for containers and per-container find() for logs. Fine for small datasets but will degrade at scale; consider aggregation pipeline.
Uses deprecated FastAPI on_event('startup'/'shutdown') hooks. Migrate to lifespan handlers (FastAPI >=0.93).
JWT_EXPIRES_DAYS=30 is long; no refresh-token / revocation mechanism. Acceptable for MVP.

action_items: Optional: tighten Pydantic models to enforce non-empty bl_number/supplier_name/country (min_length=1, strip_whitespace=True).
Optional: migrate startup/shutdown to FastAPI lifespan handlers.
Optional: optimise /api/dashboard/summary with $lookup aggregation to avoid N+1 queries.
critical_code_review_comments: All routes properly prefixed with /api and all responses exclude Mongo _id via projections — good.
JWT auth pattern (HTTPBearer + jwt.decode) is clean; bcrypt with $2b$ hashes verified via login flow.
User isolation enforced at every endpoint via user_id filter — verified by tests (userB cannot read userA purchases/containers).
Cascade delete on /api/purchases/{id} correctly removes containers + log_measurements.
CBM/CFT formulas match spec: CBM = LE*G*G/16_000_000, CFT = CBM*35.315 — verified via tests.
sr_no auto-increment continues correctly when adding new_containers via PATCH.
container_status_for() recomputes status from logs + is_loading_complete each time — correct but could be cached.
Indexes created on startup are correct (unique username; compound unique user_id+bl_number).
test_credentials: smoke_user / pass123 verified via /api/memory/test_credentials.md. Tests create fresh TEST_* users per run for isolation.
seed_data_creation: Each test run creates two fresh users (TEST_a_*, TEST_b_*) and a few BL/containers with TEST_* prefixes; no cleanup performed (data is namespaced by random UUIDs and per-user).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is fully verified at https://log-tracker-28.preview.emergentagent.com/api. Re-run pytest /app/backend/tests/test_timber_backend.py for regression. If frontend testing is needed next, key flows are: signup/login -> Purchases page (add BL with 1+ containers) -> Measurements page (select BL, select container, enter LE1/L/G1/G2 — verify live CBM/CFT, girth<35cm warning, mark-complete toggle, bulk save) -> Dashboard (filters bl_search/country/date_from/date_to, BL-grouped expand, Excel export client-side). Bottom nav on mobile (<=768px), top nav on desktop. Token stored in localStorage as 'tl_token'.
